ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bubble tea entry; no specific milk-free less-sugar variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific bubble tea without milk less-sugar code exists. Closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for milk-free less-sugar bubble tea; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: tea base (black tea or green tea or fruit tea)

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium cup of bubble tea without milk at less-sugar level, approximately 500 g, as commonly served at Singaporean bubble tea outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Bubble Tea (No Milk, Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D08010004 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this canonical HPB entry. Sodium was reported as -1 in the source, indicating the value was not published in the HPB record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sugar-Sweetened Beverages and Tea-Based Drinks — analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened tea beverage data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for milk-free less-sugar bubble tea in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated by analogy to sucrose-sweetened tea beverages (GI ~65–72) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database. The less-sugar formulation reduces sugar content but does not materially alter the GI of the sugar fraction present. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 30.35) / 100 ≈ 21.2, based on 500 g serving with 6.07 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar present with negligible fibre and potassium; sodium data unavailable but beverage is not a significant sodium source
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium data is unavailable for this beverage, the primary hypertension concern is the presence of added sugar syrup (3.17 g/100 g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g) and low potassium, which does not support blood pressure management; individuals with hypertension should choose genuinely unsweetened tea with zero added sugar.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with high GI (70) and negligible fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: This less-sugar bubble tea remains a sugar-sweetened beverage with a high glycemic index of 70 and only 0.03 g dietary fibre per 100 g, meaning the 3.17 g sugar per 100 g is rapidly absorbed and will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage and choose genuinely unsweetened tea instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; low calorie density; added sugar is the primary cardiovascular concern at this consumption level
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains negligible fat (0 g/100 g) and saturated fat (0 g/100 g), posing minimal direct cardiovascular lipid risk, but the presence of added sugar syrup at 3.17 g/100 g contributes to excess caloric intake and triglyceride elevation if consumed regularly; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit sugar-sweetened beverages and prefer unsweetened tea.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production; negligible anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The added sugar in this less-sugar bubble tea promotes inflammatory mediator production relevant to rheumatoid arthritis, and the beverage provides no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients such as omega-3 fatty acids or polyphenols in sufficient quantity; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should replace this with genuinely unsweetened green tea to obtain polyphenol benefit without any sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bubble Tea without Milk (Less Sugar) is a freshly prepared tea-based or fruit-based beverage popular across Singapore and Southeast Asia, served without any dairy or plant-based milk component and with a reduced quantity of sugar syrup compared to the standard formulation. The beverage is typically made from a base of black tea, green tea, or fruit-flavoured tea, shaken with ice and a smaller measure of sugar syrup, and optionally topped with tapioca pearls (boba), grass jelly, aloe vera, or other customisable toppings. The texture is light and refreshing with a mildly sweet flavour profile; the tea character is more pronounced than in full-sugar variants. Per 100 g, this beverage provides 24 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 6.07 g carbohydrate, 3.17 g sugar, and 0.03 g dietary fibre. Protein and fat are naturally zero as this is a tea-and-sugar-syrup beverage with no dairy or protein-containing ingredients in the base formulation; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. Sodium data was not published in the HPB source record. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, this beverage retains a high glycemic index of approximately 70 and remains classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage. It is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation from liquid sugar with negligible fibre buffering. For hypertension, the added sugar with low potassium and fibre does not support blood pressure management. For cardiovascular disease, the fat profile is benign but regular consumption of added sugar contributes to triglyceride elevation. The beverage is vegan, dairy-free, halal, and gluten-free in its base form. It is commonly consumed as a snack or breakfast beverage in Singapore, Taiwan, and across Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with review_status reviewed and confidence 0.95 in the canonical input record. The food is an exact match to the HPB entry D08010004. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this specific variant.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as this is a tea-and-sugar-syrup beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients in the base formulation; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D08010004). fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation as no fat-containing ingredients are present in the base beverage; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. sodium_mg is set to -1: the HPB Singapore Foodo source record for D08010004 did not publish a sodium value for this item; sodium data is unavailable in the HPB database for this specific beverage as of the accessed date. potassium_mg, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this freshly prepared tea beverage variant are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened tea beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for milk-free less-sugar bubble tea. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 500 g serving size: (70 × 30.35) / 100 = 21.2 g. The 'less sugar' designation in Singapore typically corresponds to approximately 50% of the standard sugar quantity used by the outlet.
